claims.claim: Zero-knowledge proofs / ZK infrastructure (zkEVMs, succinct verifiability)
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Account abstraction / smart wallets (ERC-4337, programmable accounts, social recovery)
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: US regulation-by-enforcement as biggest risk to competitiveness
claims.label: absent

claims.claim: Speculation over utility (meme / SocialFi froth) burns trust and distracts builders
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: ETH as the settlement/security base asset
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Neutral risk regime: cautious on price beta, constructive on fundamentals, build through cycles
claims.label: confirmed

missed_changes: Heavy, sustained emphasis on AI-content provenance + proof-of-personhood as the central thesis, repeatedly endorsing Worldcoin/World ID as the sybil-resistance solution (more specific and intense than the prediction's general framing)
New emphasis on formal verification / smart-contract security tooling (Halmos symbolic tests, Security Alliance, Whitehat Safe Harbor Agreement) — not anticipated by the prediction
New applied focus on crypto/stablecoin payments (USDC) as a wedge against Apple/Google App Store fees, plus mobile crypto wallets/browsers and EU DMA implications
Framing 'compute' / high-quality block space as the primary binding constraint on crypto apps (UX, onchain games, graph data structures, complex markets)
Active engagement with DAO legal-entity primitives (DUNA) and on-chain gaming (Primodium, EVE), which the prediction only flagged as lower-conviction watch items
notes: All five excitement sectors are confirmed, most strongly by Eddy's own authored a16z 'big ideas' entry on smart accounts/passkeys/MPC/embedded wallets (account abstraction) and by repeated ZK work (Jolt, SNARKs-on-ARM, Naysayer proofs, FDE) and AI-provenance posts. L2/modular confirmed via Helios light client, Magi/Optimism, Celo L2, EIP-4844 blobs. Identity confirmed via programmable private decentralized ID and trust-graph + sybil-resistance commentary. ETH judged confirmed at stance level — he never discusses ETH-the-asset's issuance/staking, but his work stays firmly Ethereum-centric, affirming Ethereum as the base layer. Of the three concerns, only anti-speculation (memecoin critique, 24 Mar 2024) is confirmed; he never voices the sequencer/bridge-centralization concern, and the regulation concern is absent — his one regulatory remark ('regulatory strategies improving') is mildly optimistic rather than the predicted alarm. Risk regime confirmed: consistently build-through-cycles, fundamentals-over-beta, never chasing price.
